title: A Look at Treasure by Torchlight - a light and simple solo dungeon crawler
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gENAeokhMHI

Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews Treasure by Torchlight, a light solo dungeon crawler from Dr. Trash Games on The Game Crafter (a review copy). You play one of three charming animal adventurers — Veronica Vole, Millie Mole or Axel Axolotl — building randomly generated dungeons from interlocking puzzle-piece tiles, fighting tiered monsters, grabbing treasure, levelling up via slotted modular character boards, and shopping between quests, all while a dwindling torch threatens to leave you fighting in the dark. Defeated foes can be banked as XP or spent to recharge special abilities — a currency choice Daniel particularly likes — and a series of escalating quests builds toward an 18-HP warlord boss.

He finds genuine charm and a solid foundation here: quick play, cute characters, a well-written rulebook. But his main reservation is cost — at $65 the over-produced components (laser-etched puzzle tiles, an elaborate screwed-together modular board) push the price past what he can recommend; at around $40 it'd be a light buy. He'd also love more variety: extra bosses to shuffle, an NPC-escort side quest, and especially a tweaked, more puzzle-like tile-placement rule that forbids running off the grid. A warm look at a promising prototype he'd encourage the designer to refine and reprice.

----

Daniel argues Treasure by Torchlight would shine if it dropped its pricey deluxe components for something cheaper and simpler. Do you prefer a small crawl produced lavishly, or stripped down so it costs half as much?
